data.title: The U.S. government's efficiency department is targeting the military sector, and the Department of Defense has listed a list of employees to be laid off
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to market news, the U.S. government's efficiency department has set its sights on the military field, and the U.S. Department of Defense has listed a list of employees to be fired. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Government Targets Military Sector for Efficiency Improvements
data.description: According to Odaily, market reports indicate that the U.S. Department of Defense has compiled a list of employees for potential dismissal as part of efforts by the U.S. Government Efficiency Department to enhance efficiency within the military sector.

data.title: Norwegian Authorities Charge Four Men In Alleged Crypto Fraud Scheme
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Norwegian authorities have charged four men in connection with an alleged cryptocurrency investment scheme that defrauded thousands of investors and laundered the proceeds through a law firm's accounts. Norway’s National Authority for Investigation and Prosecution of Economic and Environmental Crime, known as Økokrim, announced on February 16 that the scheme reportedly amassed over 900 million Norwegian kroner, equivalent to $80 million, from victims across the globe.

Økokrim state prosecutor Joakim Ziesler Berge described the operation as a significant and widespread fraud, highlighting the large number of victims from various countries who lost their investments. The victims were reportedly misled into believing they were investing in a lucrative business with substantial investments in sectors such as gas, mining, and real estate. However, Økokrim claims that the scheme made no real investments and generated no profits, relying instead on existing investors to recruit new ones.

The investigation revealed that over 700 million Norwegian kroner, or approximately $62 million, was allegedly laundered through the accounts of a local law firm and several companies in Asia. The complexity of the scheme was compounded by the use of client accounts and company structures both in Norway and internationally, making it challenging to trace the funds.

The state-run Norwegian Broadcasting Corporation (NRK) reported on February 17 that the accused are Norwegian men in their 50s, 60s, and 70s, who allegedly operated the scheme from March 2015 to November 2018. Three of the men face charges related to collecting the funds, while the fourth is accused of aiding in money laundering.

Christian Flemmen Johansen, representing one of the defendants, stated that his client denies any involvement in the scheme. Similarly, lawyer Ole Petter Drevland, defending another accused, asserted that his client rejects any criminal responsibility. Information regarding the legal representation of the other two defendants was not immediately available.

The case is scheduled to be heard in Oslo District Court in September and is expected to last over 60 days.

data.title: Bankless Ventures announces Fund II with $50 million target
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Bankless Ventures announced the launch of Fund II, with a target of raising US$50 million, and plans to add an investment partner to find excellent founding teams and transactions.
In addition, Bankless Ventures Fund I has completed 90% of the capital deployment, with a scale of US$40 million.
It believes that the frontier areas of the future crypto industry include AI agents, stablecoins, and ZK technology, and Bankless Ventures will deploy capital in these areas.

data.title: Charles Schwab Appoints Digital Asset Head, Plans Crypto Trading Services
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on February 19, Nate Geraci, President of The ETF Store, announced on social media that Charles Schwab, a leading brokerage firm in the United States, has established a position for a digital asset head. The company is expected to offer cryptocurrency spot trading services.

data.title: BNB Drops Below 640 USDT with a 3.82% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 19, 2025, 01:16 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 640 USDT and is now trading at 639.840027 USDT, with a narrowed 3.82% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Talus Integrates Sui to Power Next-Generation AI Agents and Leverages Walrus for On-Chain Storage
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Sui announced a partnership with Talus, an on-chain AI agent platform. According to reports, Talus enables developers to launch tokenized AI agents, automate trading strategies, and design autonomous economies that operate around the clock.
Talus AI agents leverage Sui's high-speed, low-cost infrastructure to be able to develop, optimize, and function in real time. As part of the plan, Talus will launch its on-chain agent framework Nexus on Sui and debut its AI consumer platform Idol.fun.
Through its Nexus framework, Talus enables developers to build composable AI agents that execute workflows directly on the chain, enabling secure, transparent, and verifiable AI automation.
In addition to integrating with Sui, Talus has also selected Walrus as its default decentralized storage platform. Walrus is a decentralized storage protocol that allows any application to publish, read, and program any data type on-chain. It can support Talus in many ways, including: storage of large AI models, data retrieval to assist decision-making, and providing trusted on-chain history for artificial intelligence agents.

data.title: The Reserve Bank of New Zealand cut interest rates by 50 basis points as expected
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The Reserve Bank of New Zealand cut its benchmark interest rate by 50 basis points to 3.75%, in line with market expectations and marking the fourth consecutive meeting to cut interest rates. (Jinshi)

data.title: Binance Launches Write to Earn Sharing Campaign to Win BNB Rewards
data.description: According to the official announcement, Binance Square announced the launch of the WritetoEarn sharing event. Participants are required to share their WritetoEarn experience on the X platform and attach the event link. The event time is from 00:00 (UTC) on February 19, 2025 to 23:59 (UTC) on February 27, 2025. Participants are required to submit up to 3 post links. The content must be original and related to the WritetoEarn experience. After the event, the 10 participants with the highest number of views will each receive 1BNB reward. (This newsletter is generated with the assistance of AI)

data.title: FT: Trump considers 25% tariffs on imported cars, medicines and chips
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Trump is considering imposing tariffs of about 25% on cars, medicines and chips imported into the United States. Speaking of chips and medicines, Trump said: "Tariffs will be at or above 25%, and they will rise significantly this year." Further tariff increases may be made later this year in order to give companies "a little chance" to move their operations back to the United States. 
He added that the final details of the auto tariffs "may" be announced on April 2, after he had previously said the tariffs would take effect on that date. (FT)
